figures.caption: Table 4: Generation Accuracy (%) / Inference Time (s) for n bits=2 quantization on Three Machines for SingleHeadQLlamaModel / MultiHeadsQLlamaModel models running in the FHE mode (execute).

Fig. 4: Class hierarchy of the proposed FHE-enabled QLLaMA-3 models. Encrypted attention modules inherit from the original LLaMA-3 architecture while selectively replacing attention heads with quantized and FHE-compatible counterparts. The classes in darker grey represents the main executable model variants of PQC-LLAMA-3.
Table 1: Hardware Specifications of both machines, running on an Ubuntu 22.04.4 LTS operating system.
Table 6: Assessment of FHE protections for common attacks in LLM deployment.
Table 2: Generation Accuracy (%) / Inference Time (s) for n bits=2 integer quantization on Three Machines for the SingleHeadQLlamaModel running in two modes (simulate/execute).

Fig. 6: An overview of the PQC-LLaMA-3 model for inference. It processes the input until the first layer locally, then sends it to the server side to homomorphically evaluate the attention mechanism. The evaluation result is sent back to the local machine to continue the inference process. This is a model describing an efficient use case of our model within a client-server model where the server has powerful hardware to run the expensive homomorphic operations on the attention mechanism.
Table 3: Generation Accuracy (%) / Inference Time (s) for n bits=2 integer quantization on Three Machines for the MultiHeadsQLlamaModel running in two modes (simulate/execute).
Fig. 5: Programmable Bootstrapping Evaluation Circuit formed of univariate and linear functions. It reduces noise per function (Evaluation + Bootstrapping)
